technical_terms_dropped: pūjārhau (rendered as 'genuinely worthy of veneration' — preserved)
iṣubhiḥ pratiyotsyāmi (the counter-fight imagery — softened to 'touch this code')
verdict_reason: The engineering layer does not invoke a sectarian-specific reading of the verse. It uses the consensus reading (Arjuna's objection is sincere but functions as deferral; misplaced loyalty as paralysis-mechanism). Shankara and Ramanuja both treat the objection as sincere but framed wrongly — the engineering layer matches this consensus. The mohāt (delusion-of-loyalty) reading common to both major traditions is preserved by the engineering claim that the objection 'sounds like ethics, feels like ethics, but functions as deferral.' No technical Sanskrit term doctrinally load-bearing in the verse has been silently dropped — pūjārhau is functionally preserved. The 'arrows in battle' imagery is softened to 'touch this code,' which is acceptable cross-domain rendering. PASS.
